description_long: This is a large, four-engine military bomber aircraft with a straight, mid-mounted wingspan and a robust, cylindrical fuselage. The wings are adorned with roundels and feature four prominent engine nacelles, each housing a propeller. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The fuselage displays military markings, including the code 'RF398' and roundels, indicating its likely use in historical or military contexts. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed, with multiple windows for visibility. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A large, four-engine military bomber with a straight mid-mounted wing, cylindrical fuselage, and conventional tail, marked with roundels and 'RF398'. The forward, enclosed cockpit and four propeller engines are prominent features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four propeller engines, straight wings, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Side/top view highlighting the 'RF398' marking, roundels on the wings, and the full length of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetrical engine arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side/top view with clear visibility of the cockpit, engine nacelles, and military markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear, configuration not fully visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
